checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=195247000 (2010-03-31) growing to 258294000 (2011-03-31), a 32% increase, while SalesRevenueNet grew from 1966748000 (FY2010) to 2362886000 (FY2011), a 20% increase
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=223104000 (2012-03-31) declining to 179565000 (2013-03-31), while SalesRevenueNet fell from 2316203000 (FY2012) to 2099883000 (FY2013)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=64957000 (FY2010, 2009-04-01 to 2010-03-31) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=365259000 (FY2010)

NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=71458000 (FY2012) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=196142000 (FY2012); NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-228137000 (FY2013) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=116990000 (FY2013)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=280814000 (2011-03-31) growing to 297072000 (2012-03-31) while SalesRevenueNet declined from 2362886000 (FY2011) to 2316203000 (FY2012)

GrossProfit=836506000/SalesRevenueNet=2362886000 = 35.4% (FY2011) vs GrossProfit=776589000/SalesRevenueNet=2316203000 = 33.5% (FY2012) vs GrossProfit=707302000/SalesRevenueNet=2099883000 = 33.7% (FY2013)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-37365000 (Q1 FY2010, 2009-04-01 to 2009-06-30) vs NetIncomeLoss=107032000 (FY2009 annual) vs NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-228137000 (FY2013 annual)
OperatingIncomeLoss=-59280000 (Q1 FY2013, 2012-04-01 to 2012-06-30) then OperatingIncomeLoss=-180017000 (Q3 FY2013, 2012-10-01 to 2012-12-31)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=5870000 (2010-03-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=195247000 (2010-03-31), a coverage ratio of about 3.0%
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2153000 (2013-03-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=179565000 (2013-03-31), a coverage ratio of about 1.2%
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent declined every year: 5870000 (2010-03-31), 4086000 (2011-03-31), 2472000 (2012-03-31), 2153000 (2013-03-31)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filing_date=2009-08-06 followed by 10-Q filing_date=2009-08-10
10-Q/A filing_date=2010-08-25 (accession 0001032975-10-000019) amending the quarter ended 2010-06-30
10-Q/A filing_date=2011-09-06 (accession 0001193125-11-241116) amending the quarter ended 2011-06-30

8-K/A amendments filed on 2009-01-20, 2010-02-19, and 2012-06-08, plus recurring CORRESP/UPLOAD (SEC comment letter) correspondence in 2006, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012 and CORRESP filed 2013-01-22 shortly before the 8-K filed 2013-01-24 announcing the goodwill impairment

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for SalesRevenueNet, GrossProfit, OperatingIncomeLoss, NetIncomeLoss/NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic, Assets, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent spanning fiscal years 2009-2013
NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities=455000 (2011-04-01 to 2011-06-30) is the last populated data point for that series, with no FY2012/FY2013 figures provided

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible delayed/insufficiently conservative goodwill impairment testing in FY2010-FY2012, allowing goodwill to remain at elevated carrying value (Goodwill=560523000 at 2012-03-31; Goodwill=561080000 at 2012-09-30) before a sudden, very large non-cash impairment was recognized in Q3 FY2013 (Goodwill=345313000 at 2012-12-31), driving OperatingIncomeLoss=-180017000 (Q3 FY2013, 2012-10-01 to 2012-12-31) and NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-228137000 (FY2013 annual). This 'big bath' pattern suggests prior-period asset values and earnings may have been overstated relative to underlying economics that had already begun deteriorating (SalesRevenueNet=2316203000 FY2012 vs 2362886000 FY2011).

Reduction of bad debt allowance as a percentage of gross receivables (from ~3.0% to ~1.2% over FY2010-FY2013) could reflect reserve releases that modestly understate the allowance for doubtful accounts, thereby flattering net receivables and, through the bad-debt expense line, net income in the periods where the ratio declined.

Inventory build-up in FY2012 (InventoryNet=297072000 at 2012-03-31 vs 280814000 at 2011-03-31) despite a revenue decline (SalesRevenueNet=2316203000 FY2012 vs 2362886000 FY2011) could indicate delayed recognition of inventory obsolescence/excess reserves, deferring cost of goods sold recognition and temporarily supporting gross margin (33.5% FY2012) until later write-downs.

top_signals: Large, sudden non-cash goodwill impairment (~$216M) in Q3 FY2013 following several years of flat/growing goodwill despite already-declining revenue, raising questions about the timeliness of prior impairment testing (Goodwill=561080000 at 2012-09-30 vs 345313000 at 2012-12-31; NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=-228137000 FY2013)

Persistent decline in allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables from ~3.0% to ~1.2% (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=5870000 at 2010-03-31 to 2153000 at 2013-03-31)

Filing chronology irregularities: an NT 10-Q late filing (2009-08-06), two consecutive-year 10-Q/A restatements of Q1 filings (2010-08-25 and 2011-09-06), multiple 8-K/A amendments, and recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence cycles through 2013

Earnings/cash-flow quality otherwise appears reasonably sound: operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income, and receivables/margins did not show sustained divergence from revenue, tempering overall risk to a 'watch' rather than 'elevated' tier
